entry.title: Re: Memory protection fault in OLE container control - Ad...
entry.content: Yes, it got started so suddenly in no time. previously it worked properly.

entry.title: Re: #COE#BUMA - How to disable SAP system notifications for feedback
entry.content:
To assist you better, I might need to ask a few clarifying questions:

1.Which RPA Tool Are You Using?

2.How Are the Notifications Delivered? Are the notifications you're receiving pop-up messages within the SAP GUI, email notifications, or something else?

3.When Do These Notifications Occur? Do these notifications appear at specific times or during particular transactions or processes?

4.Have You Tried Any Suppression Methods?Have you attempted any methods to suppress these notifications, such as modifying user parameters or changing system settings?

5.Impact on Processes:How critical is it to suppress these notifications immediately? Is it causing process failures, delays, or data inconsistencies?

6.Configuration of BOT User.Is the BOT user configured differently from a regular user in terms of authorizations or system settings? or Special configurations might help in fine-tuning system behavior for the BOT.

***Potential Solutions Based on General Use Cases:

  • Suppressing SAP GUI Messages:
  • Use SAP GUI scripting to capture and dismiss unwanted pop-ups.
  • Running in Background Mode:
  • Ensure your BOT processes are scheduled as background jobs, minimizing the chance of encountering interactive prompts.

Feel free to provide additional details or answer the questions above so that I can give more targeted advice!

entry.title: Re: CAP - CDS REPL to connect to db {kind : hana}
entry.content: This works for me all the time. What does `cds.env.profiles` and `cds.env.requires.db` return in `repl`?

entry.title: Hiring for SAP SuccessFactors/HCM Consultants & Managers - Big Four - Belgium (Permanent)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am currently working with a Partner for Human Capital with one of the Big Four. They require SuccessFactors Consultants as well as SAP HCM Consultants across various seniorities. Within this business, they offer a clear promotion path into leadership if this is an area you wish to grow into, as well as ample opportunities to obtain certifications and relevant training.

As expected within the Big Four, they work with global customers and large-scale transformation projects, many of which are greenfield implementations of SuccessFactors modules. 

Salaries range from €61,000 to €80,000 + all additional benefits, they are also able to optimize the net salaries well. You must be living in Belgium for these opportunities and speak Dutch or French.

For more information, please email me at alfie.lampe@empiric.com or connect with me on LinkedIn (@alfielampe).

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy iFlow in Integration Suite for Azure...
entry.content: Thanks for the info. In the blog it seems more like an optional function and not a mandatory one. It works with this change

entry.title: Re: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel
entry.content:
Hi Nikhil, Open and save workbooks on SAC was released in the August 2024 update.

You should find the buttons in the ribbon now. 

Regards, Steffen

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

the question is: Did it work before 24.08.0 ? We would need to know when it "stopped" working if you suspect a change on our end cause this. However - either way i would kindly ask you to create a support ticket here and mention that i (Franzisca Zarrai) recommended you create a ticket so that support knows. Also post the link to this conversation here and as much detail as you can provide:

  • How are you trying to use the keyboard/add it to FSM?
  • Did it ever work? If yes with which version of the mobile app did it stop working? Or was it an OS update after which it stopped working?

The more details you provide, the better the odds are that we can help you with this.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy iFlow in Integration Suite for Azure...
entry.content: Ok great, this is more like a security best-practice to not put credentials directly in your flows, but manage them centrally and securely. You will see this concept in multiple places in Integration Suite.

Connect with me on LinkedIn

entry.title: Purchase Requisition User Exit
entry.content:
Hi All,

When creating a PR, I want to map the email address assigned to the SAP ID of the PR requisitioner to the Email field under Item -> Delivery Address -> Address Details -> Communication. I looked into enhancement MEREQ001 but couldn't find a variant that allows assigning to the Communication Email. Has anyone encountered this issue before or can provide some guidance on where to look?

Thanks

entry.title: User is not able to open the longtext word document in Work Order header

User is not able to open the longtext word document in Work Order header. Once the user opens the longtext word document in the work order header the document will open and closes automatically and the screen will move to the text editor format. It is happening only for one user. Even we tried reinstalling the application but still facing the same issue.

Regards,

Preethi

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content: Hi Franzisca, thanks for the reply

entry.title: Re: Configuration Tab missing in PDS screen (/n/SAPAPO/CU...
entry.content:
Hi,

Is the product VC or CDP relevant?
Is the product set as a KMAT or a variant?
You need to ensure that if you have a KMAT that and your use e.g CDP configuration that the CDP class is assigned and the so called flagclass field is set correct.


There are KBAs for that which can help so correct maybe incorrect product master data in PPDS.
see e.g. KBA https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3456991  Wrong Configuration Relevancy on PPDS Product Master in ePPDS or DSC system

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2927766  - Missing class type 230 and 400 on S4HANA PPDS system.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/cdp-class-tab-not-populated-in-product-master-even-though-it-is-maintained/qaq-p/12747972

entry.title: Re: Third-Party Keyboards FSM [iOS]
entry.content:
Hi Franzisca, thanks for the reply.

I created case 835635/2024: Not possible to add third party keyboard in FSM Mobile App.
Regarding the version, I know it was working around the 23rd of April or something like that, because we were testing third party keyboards then.

Now we created our own numeric keyboard and we are not able to add it to the mobile app.

Kind regards

Laurens

entry.title: Re: Migrating Workflows to Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I have checked the "What's new" section for the workflow management, BPA and Product Lifecycle Management and i see there indeed hasn't been an update on the 16th of August.

But we came across this article Migration Article in which it is stated:
"Starting with the July 2024 delivery of SAP Product Lifecycle Management, it is mandatory for you to migrate from SAP Workflow Management service to SAP Build Process Automation if you want to continue viewing existing workflows, creating new workflows for collaborations."

Just to make sure, my question is: if we want to use only BPA without BAS, is it possible to import old workflows AND update them inside the BPA, or do we have to build new ones in BPA ?

entry.title: Re: How to configure XSUAA in CAP + MDK?
entry.content: Hi Emorales, I would suggest you to port your CAP OData V4 service into a LCAP project in BAS, and also create a MDK UI app in it, and then deploy the project to BTP, LCAP deployment module will take care all the security configuration automatically for you, you can compare the configuration of the LCAP project with the one of your current project to see any differences.

entry.title: When creating a New Cancellation Invoice, the Posting Date field is grayed out.
entry.content:
When we try to reverse few customer invoices the "Posting Date" field in the reversal document cannot be changed. 
For other invoices from a closed posting period, the field still appears to be changeable. Also set the field to editable for invoices from open posting periods.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: When creating a New Cancellation Invoice, the Posting...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

The system behavior is correct since the posting period is currently in status Open and Not blocked.

If the posting period is open and an invoice in that particular period is canceled, the Posting Date field in the new cancellation invoice will be grayed out and read-only.

It assures that the invoice and the cancellation invoice fall into the same accounting period.

To be able to change the posting date, you need to close the period following the steps below:

  1. Go to the General Ledger work center.
  2. Choose the Companies view.
  3. Select the company ID XYZ (XYZ represents the ID of the company)
  4. Click Set Process Control.
  5. Select the relevant Process Control and change the Process Control Status to Blocked.
  6. Click Save.

After that, you are able to change the posting date in the cancellation invoice.

Regards, 

Arjun

entry.title: Infotype 0008: Can I block specific fields for enter information that are related to infotype 0007?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Is it possible to block fields in infotype 0008 to enter information? We experience different information regarding work time between IT0008 and IT0007. People ignore the hint when updating information in IT0007 that the information needs to be changed in IT0008 as well.

Is it either possible to display the information from IT0007 in the view of IT0008 and prevent changes her? Or is there any other good solution? Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards,

Ulrike

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi,

Post the data preview of zhelp_new5. The annotation syntax looks fine.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud & SAP Document Management Service, Integration Option - External Repository
entry.content: I set up my S/4HANA Cloud with an internal DMS repo, but when I go to attach a document o a business partner, it says it cannot reach it. Is there any trace or way to know where it failed (e.g., bad credentials, bad repo, maybe it did not even get to calling the API).

entry.title: Re: Input Control and Filtering - Unbooked data
entry.content:
At the moment there is no validation rules between dimensions like you require, but I'm aware of an idea enhancement for this so hopefully it will arrive some day.

In the meantime, one approach is to create an input story (for administrators) on a separate part of the data model (e.g. on a dummy version). You would enter the value of e.g. "1" into the correct combinations of accounts and legal entities. 

Then in your story, you can bring in the dummy version into the 1st column of your table (you may even be able to hide with right click/hide member), and then you set it to booked data only. This should then show all of the combinations you require per legal entity selected.

entry.title: Re: Record in lookup table data ignored during subscription price calculation - Subscription billing

Hi Carsten,

thank you (again 😊) for the clear explanation.

This is very important information when setting up the pricing configuration to avoid "polluting" the system with redundant settings.

thank you for your support and experience.
Kr,
Elena

entry.content:
Hello all,

Since a few days ago whenever I go into any SCN post using Firefox the tab keeps refreshing infinitely. At first I thought it was an issue with my mouse but it doesn't happen with Edge or Chrome.

Today I deleted all cookies, temp files and history and still happens.

Is anyone else experiencing the same issue? 

Thanks in advance 🙂

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
@gerd_schoeffl 

Just FYI I've added a new enhancement request for 2 features I believe should be available - clean/replace version and clean/replace subset of data. At the moment with the widget there's no way to clear data debris if a user is uploading data to an area with existing data. This would require a separate data action to do (but we cannot also yet embed file upload starters into multi-actions).

All - please vote on the enhancement request if you would like this feature too!

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/329138

I also agree that the fiscal year setting would be great to have as for some clients this would be needed to be able to use this functionality, plus the master data creation option. 

 

David

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Thanks @comercial_cinfa for the reply. It will be supported in future releases. 

we will evaluate to support the Bookmark options. But after applying filters (data point selection) it will create a new state (similar to bookmark) in PPT and one click of refresh will bring you the latest data.

Regards,

Ashok R

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge

entry.title: Error Update statistics of table failed in ATM Logs (ASE db)
entry.content:
Hello,

I get the following error message for different tables in my ATM logs in the DBACOCKPIT: Update statistics of table failed

The tables are both in saptools and in the area in which the data from the tables in SAP are located. The db is an Sybase ASE db (version: 16.0.04.04). The SAP version is 753 

I couldn't find any noticeable consequences but would like to resolve these errors. Does anyone know why this happens and how I can solve it?

I could only find solutions for this error if the update is executed manually.

Thank you

Ajay

entry.title: Host Alias in SAP Integration Suite Trial
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently trying to use the SAP Integration Suite Trial to work trough different tutorials. In particular I tried the following two tutorials:

  • https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-with-sap-integration-suite/creating-an-api_b3973a9a-62ce-4a5e-b59a-c0ddf6924de5
  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-apim-create-api.html

The problem with the Integration Suite Trial is, that the Host Alias, which is generated for an API Proxy, can not be changed. At least I didn't find any option to do so. As a result it is not possible to follow the two tutorials mentioned above completely.

I found the following question related to the issue: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-learning-q-a/developing-with-sap-integration-suite-how-to-modify-the-api-proxy-host-name/qaq-p/224504. This post shows that it is possible to invoke the API Proxy by manually adjusting the URL in the test environment.

Is there any way to change the Host Alias for a generated API Proxy in the trail environment?

Christian

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

My name is Soumya Chakraborty, I am a student who's trying to shift his career trajectory and being immensely interested in SAP and after working on a project based on creating a business blueprint for a company for their SAP implementation, I want to become certified with SAP and take on my professional journey. I have recently completed the TS410 course(Integrated Business Process) through the Digital Skills Initiative, which I found incredibly valuable. However, I am in a challenging situation and need your assistance.

Previously, I attempted the certification exam and unfortunately scored 58%, just shy of the required 61% for passing. This has been weighing heavily on me, as I am truly dedicated to advancing my skills and contributing effectively to my career. I am confident that if given another opportunity, I can successfully pass the exam and achieve the certification.

Due to some pressing personal and professional reasons, it is crucial for me to clear this certification at the earliest as my placement for my professional degree in starting soon. I am urgently requesting your support to allow me to retake the exam under the initiative mentioned on the website. I assure you that this time, I will put in the extra effort needed to succeed.

entry.title: Re: Service Layer Single Sign On (SSO) example or tutorial
entry.content:
Hi MDIAS,

The explanation is provided in reference to Service Layer only.

You will not be able to login using SAP Business One user and password when SSO is enabled.

You will have to use the user created in SLD and password in order to login into Service Layer. Same user name and password which you use in SAP Business One client after enabling SSO.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: How to Block a Ship-to Location in SAP ByDesign
entry.content: Thank you for your reply. Following your instructions, I blocked the status of the Ship-to Logistics Area and created a Stock Transfer Order. I was able to create the Stock Transfer Order and the subsequent Outbound Delivery, but I couldn't select the blocked Logistics Area when creating the Inbound Delivery. With the 2408 update, isn't it possible to perform a Stock Transfer to a blocked Logistics Area? I have an obligation to verify the details of this update and explain it to the customer. Could you please provide me with step-by-step instructions on how to verify the update, along with addressing the above question? Screenshots Explanation Evidence of blocking the Logistics Area "4IDF02 10" Evidence of creating the Stock Transfer Order and the subsequent Outbound Delivery Evidence showing the error message "Logistics Area '10' cannot be used" when attempting to create the Inbound Delivery Additional Information The Logistics Model for both Inbound Delivery and Outbound Delivery is set to "Without Tasks."

entry.title: Unable to call Learning API in SAP CPI
entry.content: I want to call the following field names with value in CPI job but the learning url is not working with http adapter: https://xxxx-stage.plateau.com/learning/odatav4/public/user/PsaUserLearningHistory showing 400 error, I know this is the entity when I downloaded report with xml document so this is the entity and the oauth2 cred are working fine too, when i used odata adapter with learningHistory entity it also got some fields but when deployed gave 500 internal error. Any solution to this problem???

entry.title: Re: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel

Nikhil

entry.title: Extract Salesforce data into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence using SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Overview

To achieve end-to-end business process transformation, we at Signavio want to cover the whole process and IT  landscape of our customers including SAP and also non-SAP systems.

I am happy to share the release of our out-of-the-box content for Salesforce connectivity to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence that you can use to perform process mining analysis on Salesforce processes including Order-to-Cash.

We will go through where you can find the content and how to use it.

Check out content in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Let's have a look on how you can get started.

The content is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. On the top left corner under "Explore", view the product "SAP Signavio":

You will see on overview of all APIs and integration packages available for SAP Signavio. This includes the newly released OData API in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence under the tab "API". Check out this blog to learn more. Go to the tab "Integration":

Now you can see all available integration packages for SAP Signavio. You can now select the integration package "Salesforce Integration to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence":

Once you enter the integration package, you will see the individual integration flows. Each integration flow has its purposes with the main goal to replicate business objects and the events of the business objects from Salesforce to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. Hence, as an example, you will see a dedicated integration flow to replicate the business object "Lead" and a dedicated integration flow to replicate its events called "LeadHistory":

If you go into one specific integration flow, you will be able to see the details of the integration flow on how each step of transformation, routing, data mapping, etc. is performed. You can also download the configuration guide that details out how to deploy this integration package in your SAP Integration Suite tenant as well as view the change log for changes between released versions:

Download content into SAP Integration Suite

Now if you want to use the content, log on to SAP Integration Suite and go to "Integrations" under the tab "Discover":

If you now search for "Salesforce", you will find all available content relating to Salesforce integration, including our new package for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence:

If you check out the package "Salesforce Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence", you can additional details on the integration scenarios and which objects are included out-of-the-box. If you now want to use this package, simply click on "copy" on the top right corner to copy this integration package into your design area for further configuration and deployment.

And that is it, you did it! Let me know if you have any questions on the package and please also any additional non-SAP systems that are relevant for you to connect to SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 🙂

Here some helpful links to get you started:

SAP Signavio on SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Content: Salesforce Integration with SAP Signavio Process Intelligence

Thanks in Advance.

Lars Lutz

entry.title: Re: UDF in Landed Costs is not showing up in Service Layer
entry.content:
Hi Maria_Bocaranda,

Probably, the UDFs were not exposed in the version and patch which you are using.

I can see that the UDFs are there in the latest patch (10.0 FP2405).

Therefore, I would ask you to upgrade.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: CAP - CDS REPL to connect to db {kind : hana}
entry.content:
Hi Dinu,

Here is the output - 

 

> cds.env.profiles
[ 'development' ]
>
> cds.env.requires.db
{
  impl: '@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/hana/Service.js',
  use: [Getter],
  model: [ 'db', 'srv' ],
  connection_attempts: 3,
  kind: 'hana'
}

 

Just to inform, this is Typescript project. I get error saying 'CDS compilation failed' when I execute cds.test() or cds.connect.to('db')   

Thank you.

entry.title: Fields in Custom Business Objects

KhaingZin

entry.title: Manage Schedule [F3326] - Share schedule - Search help doesn't display Collaborator
entry.content:
Hi,

We're trying to share schedule in the app F3326 - Manage Schedule, but when we click on the Share Schedule icon and than select Maintenance Planners or Reviewer option, search help doesn't display any result. Doesn't matter if we enter our User ID, SAP User name or Full name. In each case dialog display any result.

On the Help Portal (Documentation) we found this Prerequisite:

Planners with whom you want to share a schedule must have assigned at least one work center to themselves.
Only then is the planner's name available in the Share Schedule dialog.

See link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/3da789436baf42c6a5692c4797aa00b0.html?q=collaborat
We checked it, and this is OK. I mean assigned "My Work Centers" in our user profiles.

We checked all setting described in the Implementation Information from Fiori library for this app also and everything looks OK.

Can anybody help us with this issue?

Lars Lutz

entry.title: Re: Fixed Asset Purchase IN S4 HANA Public Cloud

Hi Vijayenda

entry.title: IAS/ SSO login for user with two different SF username but same email
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We want to introduce different login method's for Admins vs  normal Users.

Current scenario : Example User John is an admin as well as an employee he has however one email address maintained in SF and two user id's a_John & P_John . He  uses  the same SF url to login into both accounts (At this stage SSO is not enabled ) and he use's userid to login .

in Future : Client will upgrade to use IAS / IPS . However in IAS unique email address is needed  hence only one account is synced with the email in IAS .

User wants a way to login into the SF system in which SSO is enabled such that he can access both his accounts via the same email . Login methods could be different for an admin id vs he as normal employee / user .

Please suggest how we can achieve this. Can we have conditional login for admins such that they have a choice to enter SF either as a admin or employee ? or can we have SSO enabled for user as a employee and disable for admins ? Please suggest Whats  are the possibilities & solutions.

Thanks,

Zeba

entry.title: SAP on Oracle RAC
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

we want to implement an SAP system on Oracle RAC with ASM on standard VMs, we have two questions.
1)In SAP note DIGITS it is reported that the Oracle RDBMS Home must be a shared file system. In our opinion this leads several limitations, including for example the impossibility of performing DB rolling Upgrades and Patches. 

Is this limitation still valid or we can implement local Oracle RDBMS Home for each node of the Oracle RAC Cluster? We are asking you because the SAP note DIGITS is not updated since DIGITS.

For "Oracle Engineered Systems" solutions this limitation does not seem to apply.

Is there any way to perform patches in rolling mode for the RDBMS Home on Oracle RAC for SAP?

2)In a RAC implementation the grid infrastructure software should be installed manually following Oracle Guides or using SAP tools or scripts provided by SAP? 

Thank you and best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP on Oracle RAC
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

1) Note 527843 is still valid. A shared file system, a cluster file system (CFS) or the Oracle ASM cluster file system (ACFS) is a prerequisite for using RAC in the SAP environment. 

For the patching you can use below note:

2799959 - Patching of Oracle Homes with Minimal Downtime (18c or higher) 

This note applies to patching of database homes, but does not apply to patching of GI homes.

2) Oracle RAC with GI should be installed by SAP tools. The installation and patches are described in below notes:

2660017 - Oracle Database Software Installation on Unix 
2799930 - Patches for 19c: Grid Infrastructure 

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Datasphere data marketplace - behind the scenes - new video for marketplace video series
entry.content:
Behind the scenes of the SAP Datasphere data marketplace

If not done before, make sure to take a look at the main blog post, containing all the previous parts of the video series!

In this video, I am talking about the technical details of the SAP Datasphere data marketplace architecture. You will see what is happening in the background when a data product is created and how we facilitate the secure delivery of the data. 

Sharing data via data products in SAP Datasphere brings you some major advantages, like:

  • having one isolated endpoint for your product artefacts
  • No need to replicate data in internal scenarios
  • Scalable data distribution
  • ability to share data within own tenant and other (ecosystem) tenants
  • active Data Access Controls 

Get all the details in this video: 

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Lucerne, Switzerland)
entry.content: Thanks for alerting us. I have fixed. The event is in that Wednesday.

entry.title: Re: New Country Code
entry.content:
Hi,

In case somebody ended up here trying to find the path to configure new countries and internal regions in S/4HANA, here is the path:

Countries:
IMG -> ABAP Platform > General settings > Set Countries > Define Countries/Regions

Internal regions:
IMG -> ABAP Platform > General settings > Set Countries > Insert Regions (Subnational Administration Units)

entry.title: Re: HCM P&F Fiori - F4 help (in a popup format)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am encountering the same problems. I want to link the country (dropdown list) with the region (input field with Matchcode). I notice that when I change the country, Fiori does not send a request to the backend, so it does not know that the instance has a different country. Moreover, I feel that even if the country is in the Where clause of the region, P&F does not take it into account.

I tried using User Events, but I did not see any difference. Do you have any documentation or examples that show how this works?

I also tried using a Generic Service, but in the GET_HELP_VALUES method, I do not have the search help filters, and I do not feel that there is an API that allows me to retrieve the filters from the OData request.

If you have any information/examples, that would be great. I am struggling to explain to my client that this basic functionality is not possible or is difficult to implement!

Help me 🙂

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration archiving with "OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions"
entry.content:
Hi all,

This blog is just to bring to light a standard archiving solution working out of the box for SAP Cloud Integration using the "OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions".

In one hand If you check carefully the documentation of such product you will notice that there is a standard "CMIS interface" to "Store & Retrieve any content", in the other hand SAP Cloud Integration uses "CMIS interface" out of the box for archiving, so why not put them together? 😉

In a nutshell the following steps need to be followed to have a successful integration:

OpenText – Core Archive - Administration ( http://<host>:<port>/archive/ba/index.jsp )
-Creation of Retention Policy ( Optional )
-Creation of Collection with CMIS Client: You will get the CMIS Browser Binding & RepositoryId - required for the SAP BTP Destination

CMIS Workbench ( in your local machine ) 
-Import custom CMIS type into OpenText Core Archive: It would be nice than SAP provided this definition, but you can extract it from the traces of Cloud Connector or OpenText Core Archive ( SAP Cloud Integration check if the CMIS type exist via APIs, in case that it does not exist it will try to create on the fly, but this will not work due to OpenText Core Archive, so it must be created manually ).
-Export custom CMIS type from OpenText Core Archive: You will extract the ID for the CMIS type ( OpenText create randoms IDs internally, it is not keeping the IDs provided for the import file ) - required for the SAP BTP Destination ( https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/configuring-destination )

SAP Cloud Connector Set-Up
-Expose the OpenText Core Archive & CMIS resources: Add the OpenText Core archive system and expose everything under "/as_cmis"

SAP – BTP – Service Instance & Service Key
-Create Destination: Just use the CMIS Browser Binding & RepositoryId, Cloud Connector Location ID, and user with write permissions, and the most important part is the additional property "CustomAttributeAndTypeNamesMapping" ( https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/configuring-destination )

SAP Cloud Integration enable archiving
-Enable archiving: enable the archiving functionality ( https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/enable-archiving-in-cloud-foundry-environment )

OpenText – Core Archive - Access ( http://<host>:<port>/archive/access/index.jsp )
-Navigate the collection: Enjoy an out of the box solution to navigate & search archived files by all the attributes enabled for it.

Not forget, be curious! 😉

Max.

entry.title: Re: How to create a new Country-Key and Region
entry.content:
Hi, In case somebody ended up here trying to find the path to configure new countries and internal regions in S/4HANA, here is the path:

Countries:

IMG -> ABAP Platform > General settings > Set Countries > Define Countries/Regions Internal regions

IMG -> ABAP Platform > General settings > Set Countries > Insert Regions (Subnational Administration Units)

Can anyone help me here with live connection here or maybe guide me towards the resolution.

Thanks,

Mayank Jha

entry.title: Re: calculate version variance dynamically based on three...
entry.content:
@Andsap918 you should create restricted Measures for  Table1 ("trend") and Table2 ("trend comparison").

And a calculated Measure Table3("trend variance")= Table1 ("trend")-Table2 ("trend comparison").

In the restricted Measures you can set a dynamic input control e.g. for version or whatever dimension you want to change.

Hope this helps.

Regards,

Ismail

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Invalid Data Entry...
entry.content: I discovered the problem. The issue was caused by entering data into the dataset that had too many # values. Once I removed those # values, everything worked fine.

entry.title: SAP Companion Integration for SAP Workzone Advance
entry.content:
I am trying to integrate SAP Companion with SAP Workzone Advance. It is working for On-Premise but not for the BTP Workzone Advance edition. Upon checking, the 'help4' variable exists as well for BTP. Would like to check if there is any mistakes with the parameters and value?

Name

Value

product

LAUNCHPADS_BTP_CF 

version

Latest 

dataUrlSEN

{manager URL}

noHelpMode

carousel

newWindowSAML

true

trackingUrlWPB

{manager URL}

serviceLayerVersion

SEN

openImmediately

minimized

entry.title: SAP Customer Checkout Manager Umzug auf neuen Server
entry.content:
In diesem Blogbeitrag erfahren Sie die Grundlagen, die für einen Umzug von einer SAP Customer Checkout Manager (CCOm) Installation auf einen anderen Server benötigt werden. Dies beinhaltet welche Voraussetzungen vorher geschaffen werden müssen, wie grundlegend vorgegangen werden muss und welche möglichen Probleme auftreten können.

Welche Informationen und Zugänge werden benötigt?

  • Zugänge zum alten und zum neuen Server
  • Admin Kennwort und Hash-Schlüssel aus der initialen Konfiguration
  • Zugriff auf die Datenbank des CCOm (SAP HANA oder MS SQL)
  • Datenbankname / Datenbankschema sollte bekannt sein (z.B. CCOS)
  • Datenbankbenutzer (z.B. Admin user/ sa user)
  • Port unter dem der alte CCOm installiert ist (z.B. 9090 oder 8080)
  • ccos.war Datei in der richtigen Version

Wie sollte vorgegangen werden?

  • Herunterfahren bzw. stoppen des Apache Tomcat Service auf dem altem Server
  • Backups erstellen
  • Kopieren der Datenbank
  • "CustomerCheckout Central Server" Ordner kopieren (C:\SAP\CustomerCheckout Central Server)
  • Installation CCOm auf neuem Server
  • Durchführen der initialen Konfiguration 
  • CCOm stoppen
  • Tauschen der Datenbank und des "CustomerCheckout Central Server" Ordners
  • CCOm starten
  • Neue Verbindungsdaten an Kassen (CCO) eintragen

 

Informationen und Zugänge

Zunächst sollte sichergestellt sein, dass die oben beschriebenen benötigten Zugänge und Verbindungsdaten vorhanden sind. Ohne das Admin Kennwort und den Hash-Key, der bei der initialen Konfiguration verwendet wurde, ist der Umzug wie hier beschrieben nicht möglich. Nützliche Informationen über die aktuell verwendete Version, die Datenbankverbindung und das verwendete Datenbankschema befinden sich im CCOm unter Konfiguration/ Information.

Abweichungen zwischen der alten und der neuen Installation sollten nach Möglichkeit vermieden werden. 

 

Vorgehen

Grundsätzlich sollte der Umzug nicht im laufenden Betrieb stattfinden. Planen Sie sich ausreichend Zeit außerhalb der Öffnungszeiten für den Umzug ein. Außerdem ist es natürlich empfehlenswert, den Prozess einmal in einer Testumgebung zu testen. Zunächst sollte der CCOm auf dem alten Server gestoppt werden. Dies kann über die Dienste geschehen. 

Anschließend sollte eine Kopie der Datenbank des CCOm gemacht werden, die bei Bedarf ebenfalls auf einem neuen Server eingespielt wird. Ebenfalls sollte ein Backup des Apache Tomcat Ordners (z.B. unter C:\Program Files\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat 9.0_Tomcat9_FP19) gemacht werden.

Die ccos.war Datei (z.B. C:\Program Files\Apache Software Foundation\Tomcat 9.0_Tomcat9_FP19\webapps\ccos.war) und der "CustomerCheckout Central Server" Ordner (z.B. C:\SAP\CustomerCheckout Central Server) werden auf den neuen Server übertragen. 

Im nächsten Schritt wird die Installation des CCOm wie bekannt durchgeführt und der gleiche Port wie bei der alten Installation gewählt. Es kann die ccos.war Datei vom alten Server oder eine neue Datei heruntergeladen werden. Wichtig ist nur, dass die gleiche Version (gleiches Feature Pack) wie bei der CCOm Installation auf dem alten Server gewählt wird. 

 

Initiale Konfiguration

Bei Durchführung der initialen Konfiguration werden die Verbindungsdaten für die entsprechende Datenbank ausgewählt und ein Benutzer der über Lese- und Schreibrechte verfügt und ein neues Schema anlegen darf (z.B. Admin/sa) hinterlegt.

Wenn der "CustomerCheckout Central Server" Ordner nicht getauscht wird, muss nicht zwangsläufig derselbe Datenbankbenutzer wie bei der ursprünglichen initialen Konfiguration, genutzt werden. Da in diesem jedoch beispielsweise die Bilder der Schnellauswahl gespeichert sind, ist ein Austausch empfehlenswert. Der Schemaname muss wiederum denselben Namen haben, wie bei der ursprünglichen Installation auf dem alten Server (z.B. CCOS). 

Setzen Sie den Haken bei "Automatically create schema (if non-existent)" und lassen so das Datenbankschema zunächst neu erstellen.

Verwenden Sie nun dasselbe Admin Passwort und Hash key, wie bei der ursprünglichen Installation des CCOm auf dem alten Server:

Klicken Sie auf OK und warten, bis die Meldung angezeigt wird, dass die Konfiguration erfolgreich war. Mit einer Anmeldung am CCOm können Sie nochmal prüfen bzw. bestätigen, dass die Installation korrekt durchgeführt wurde. 

Fahren Sie nun den Apache Tomcat wieder herunter (z.B. über die Dienste), ersetzen Sie den neuen "SAP Customer Checkout Central Server" Ordner durch den alten vom Server kopierten Ordner (sofern Sie denselben Datenbankbenutzer wie in der initialen Konfiguration verwenden) und tauschen Sie die vorher kopierte Datenbank aus.

Zum Schluss wird der CCOm wieder über die Dienste gestartet und nach einer Anmeldung geprüft, ob alle Daten (Kunden, Benutzer, Artikel, Belege...) vorhanden sind. Vergessen Sie anschließend nicht, die neuen Verbindungsdaten in der Kasse unter Konfiguration/Integration/SAP Customer Checkout manager einzutragen.
